pattern: Because the same subarray may be reused, every pick should be the single subarray of maximum spread, which is always the whole array's max minus its min.
hints: You may choose the exact same subarray more than once, so all k picks can be identical.
If all picks can be the same, you only need the single subarray with the largest possible value.
The maximum of max-minus-min over all subarrays is achieved by the whole array, so the answer is (max(nums) - min(nums)) * k.

edge_inputs.description: small array, k repeats of best subarray
wider spread, reuse the full-range value
all equal values give zero regardless of k
min and max are interior, not endpoints

stress.description: random array and k so the global spread is well-defined
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 10**9 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, 100001)
    return [nums, k]
